chapter_context.summary: Gideon is called to deliver Israel from Midianite oppression; he destroys the altar of Baal.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on Midianite Oppression (6:1-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Judges.
This section focuses on Gideon's Call (6:11-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Judges.
This section focuses on Destroying Baal's Altar (6:25-32), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Judges.
This section focuses on The Sign of the Fleece (6:33-40), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Judges.

key_themes.title: The Cycle of Sin
key_themes.description: The recurring pattern of disobedience, judgment, and rescue.

key_themes.title: Deliverance
key_themes.description: God raising up leaders to save His people despite their failures.

devotional.question: How can we recognize the 'idols' in our culture that lead us away from God?
What does the life of Samson teach us about the danger of wasting God-given potential?

cultural_historical.title: The Period of the Judges
cultural_historical.text: This era is marked by moral decay and the absence of a central king, leading to 'every man doing that which was right in his own eyes.'
